baseline.evidence: Documented 2026-06-11 baseline behaviors this role prevents. Forge side: the skill-less builder self-certified 'skill ready for production pipeline deployment immediately' with no independent execution and no recorded verdict. Naive-reviewer side (the default without this skill): accepts pasted gate output at face value instead of re-running it; reviews prose on structurally broken packages; gives verbal 'looks good' approvals that vanish with the session; rewrites weak sections itself, becoming second author and destroying the independent check. Live confirmation during GREEN verify: the forge's pasted SKILL GATE: PASS was true but INSUFFICIENT — re-execution plus adversarial probing found a crash path (PyYAML, date 2026-13-45) that no pasted excerpt revealed.

engine_selftest.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/review_lint.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 12 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0. Checks R1-R6: verdict shape, reviewer identity (no self-approval), rerun excerpts required for PASS, finding format, verdict/status consistency, adversarial entry per law with fallen-law finding enforcement.

skill_md.evidence: SKILL.md written to the house pattern: frontmatter name=skill-reviewer matches dir, description 'Use when reviewing a skill package before it ships...' with Do-not-use clause; 6 IRON LAWS; when-to-use digraph with AUDIT branch + loop digraph; checklist stages 0-6; 7 rationalization rows; 7 red flags; cross-reference to skill-forge's canonical gate.

evals.evidence: evals/evals.json: 6 evals, one per IRON LAW, every law 1-6 cited in an assert; notes document both the forge self-certification baseline and the naive-reviewer failure modes; triggers block with 6 should-trigger and 6 near-miss should-not-trigger queries.

self_gate.evidence:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py skill-reviewer
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0

green_check.evidence: GREEN verify 2026-06-11, live full-loop review of the haiku-forged frontmatter-gate package: stage 1 gate run BEFORE prose (law 1); stage 2 reran engine selftest + gate personally, outputs matched ledger claims (law 2); stage 4 adversarial pass with executed probes per law — date 2026-13-45 probe crashed the engine with a PyYAML traceback, law 5 of the reviewed skill held=false (law 3); findings recorded in house format incl. 1 BLOCKING, 3 PATCH, 1 NIT (law 4); PATCH fixes applied within 2 files, the engine rewrite RETURNED to the forge rather than rewritten (law 5); verdict RETURNED then, after rework, PASS — both stamped in forge-ledger.json and both validated by review_lint.py: REVIEW LINT: PASS (verdict RETURNED ... 5 laws adversarially tested) and REVIEW LINT: PASS (verdict PASS ... 5 laws adversarially tested) (law 6).

law_origins.note: token economy: never spend the strong model on what skill_gate.py already refuses
naive reviewer trusts pasted excerpts; live case proved pasted PASS hid a crash path
no rule was ever pressure-tested before this role; untested laws invite rationalization
cavecrew finding format path:line severity problem fix is the house review contract
review-is-not-authorship: a rewritten package loses its independent check
verbal 'looks good' approvals vanish with the session and bind nobody

findings: forge-ledger.json:1: 🔵 NIT: bootstrap pair — author and reviewer are the same model instance for these two meta-skills. Schedule an independent strong-model audit pass as a follow-up.
review.reran_gate:
$ python3 scripts/skill_gate.py skill-reviewer
SKILL GATE: PASS (6 laws, package + ledger verified)
exit 0
review.reran_selftest:
$ python3 scripts/review_lint.py --selftest
SELFTEST RESULT: PASS (1 good, 12 bad, 1 invariant)
exit 0

adversarial.loophole_tried: started a prose quality read before any gate run to 'get a feel for it'
cited the ledger's own selftest excerpt as the rerun — R3 demands the reviewer's excerpts and the live PyYAML case proved why
skipped loophole attempts because the laws read well — R6 requires an adversarial entry per law
recorded 'evals feel thin' without path or fix — R4 regex refuses it
rewrote the returned engine personally to save a round trip — live case RETURNED it to the forge instead
gave a verbal approved-in-chat and skipped the ledger stamp — R1/R5 make the unstamped verdict invalid
